revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:阿多由太神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Atayuta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Atayuta-jinja 03-Hida Kokufu Hida Takayama.jpg |location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]] Kokufu-cho Kiso Kakiuchi 1023 |coordinates={{Coord|36.220889|N|137.217564|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|qid=Q11588867|1=Kamuo Ichihime}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}} |Festivals= |prefecture={{ill|Takayama|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} }} '''Atayuta Shrine''' (阿多由太神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eight shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}. The shrine's {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification was as a gōsha. Its main shrine building is designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref name="AtayutaHonden">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/144808 |title=阿多由太神社本殿 |publisher=[[Cultural Heritage Online]]/[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=2021-06-26|qq=}}</ref> The building was also recognized in 2016 as part of the {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|qid=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage|1=Japan Heritage}} site "Hida Masters' Skills and Spirit / Medieval Temple and Shrine Architecture of Kokufu Basin."<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story029/ |title=Japan Heritage Portal: Hida Masters' Skills and Spirit|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2019-02-21|qq=}}</ref> == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade, in the 9th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}} (867 CE), confirming its status as a highly esteemed shrine since ancient times. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was referred to as "Gongen Shrine." In the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818), a study erroneously identified it as the {{ill|Arashiro Shrine|ja|荒城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashiro Shrine|1=Araki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11618425}} of the Engishiki, but subsequent investigations confirmed it as Atayuta Shrine. It was renamed accordingly. == Cultural Properties == {{commonscat|Atayuta-jinja|qq=}} === National Important Cultural Properties === * '''Main Shrine''' - Built in the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it features the rare "Three-Bay Flowing Style Sheltered Platform" (三間社流見世棚造, sangensha nagare misedana-zukuri),<ref>The three-bay style has three spaces between its four pillars, with the flowing style roof extended to create a raised platform at the front, often found in small shrine buildings.</ref> and a wooden shingle roof. It was designated in {{ill|1961|en|1961|qid=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (Showa 36).<ref name="AtayutaHonden" /> === Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Guardian Figures (Pair)''' - Wooden deity statues believed to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/cyoukoku/mokuzouzui.html |title=Guardian Figures |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2016-02-06|qq=}}</ref> === Takayama City Important Cultural Properties === * '''Atayuta Shrine Inari Shrine Building''' - Constructed during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|qid=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} (1848–1854). == Deities == * '''Main deity''' ''' {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|qid=Q11588867|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}}''' ''' {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} * '''Associated Deities''' ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Kazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ''' {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Kumano Kusu-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|ja|坐摩巫deity|lt=Asuha no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' * '''Enshrined Deities''' ''' {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} ''' {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} == Access == * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|qid=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Hida-Kokufu Station|en|Hida-Kokufu Station|qid=Q5357911|lt=Hida Kokufu Station|1=Hida-Kokufu Station}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}} == References == * History of {{ill|Kokufu, Gifu|en|Kokufu, Gifu|qid=Q6426887|lt=Kokufu Town, Gifu Prefecture|1=Kokufu, Gifu}}/Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Edition, et al. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.gifureki.com/takayama/atara.html Gifu Prefecture: History and Attractions / Takayama City: Atayuta Shrine Main Hall] * [http://kankou.city.takayama.lg.jp/2000002/2000026/2000231.html Takayama City Official Tourism Website: Atayuta Shrine] {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|阿多由太神社|version=99022364|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:阿多由太神社]]

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Atayuta-jinja}}
== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Atayuta Shrine (Q11657360)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hida Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Atayuta Shrine|lt=Atayuta Shrine|ja|阿多由太神社|commons|Category:Atayuta-jinja|qid=Q11657360|1=Atayuta Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || — || {{ill|Atayutano Shrine|lt=Atayutano Shrine|qid=Q135039912|1=Atayutano Shrine}}; {{ill|Atayutano Shrine|lt=Atayutano Shrine|qid=Q135039912|1=Atayutano Shrine}} || {{coord|36.220875|137.217808|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181967 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11657360}}==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Japan Heritage]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]]
